semantic_concept: Film festival program diversity by festival
topic_description: Compare the composition of official programs across major international film festivals by director gender, country/region of origin, and debut-feature status for a selected year or range of years.

master_data.metric_name_primary: Share (%) of films directed by women
master_data.metric_name_secondary: Share (%) of films from Global South / non-Western countries
master_data.metric_name_tertiary: Share (%) of world premieres or first-feature debuts

statistical_properties.trend_description: Two clusters: a higher cohort (Sundance, Rotterdam, Locarno, Berlinale, Busan) with notably larger shares of films directed by women and more debuts/world premieres, and a lower cohort (Cannes, Venice, TIFF, BFI, Telluride) with lower female representation. Weak-to-moderate positive relationship between female-directed share and Global South representation, with Busan and Sundance acting as notable outliers.

bar.y_label: Share of films directed by women (%)
bar.img_title: Share of Films Directed by Women Across Major Film Festivals (2023)

scatter.x_label: Share of films from Global South / non-Western countries (%)
scatter.y_label: Share of films directed by women (%)
scatter.img_title: Female-Directed Share vs Global South Representation at Major Film Festivals (2023)

pie_data_category.singular: Share of films directed by women at one festival
pie_data_category.plural: Combined shares of films directed by women across the listed festivals

pie.img_title: Distribution of Female-Directed Shares Across Major Film Festivals (2023)

bar.ground_truth_caption: The bar chart displays the share (%) of films directed by women at ten major film festivals in 2023, including Cannes, Berlin (Berlinale), Venice, Sundance, TIFF, Busan, Rotterdam, Locarno, BFI London and Telluride. Sundance has the highest share at 42.5%, followed by Rotterdam (38.1%) and Locarno (36.7%), while Telluride (16.9%) and Cannes (18.4%) show the lowest shares. Overall the values range from 16.9% to 42.5%, with several festivals clustered in the low- to mid-30% and others below 25%, indicating substantial variation and none reaching gender parity.

scatter.ground_truth_caption: The scatter plot compares ten major film festivals by share of films from the Global South/non-Western countries (x-axis) and share of films directed by women (y-axis). Busan has the highest Global South representation at 55.6% (with 33.4% female-directed), Sundance shows the highest female-directed share at 42.5% (22.1% Global South), and Rotterdam also ranks high on both metrics (48.2% Global South, 38.1% female-directed). Overall the points are dispersed—some festivals (e.g., Rotterdam, Locarno) combine relatively high Global South and female representation, while others like Telluride (12.4% Global South, 16.9% female-directed) are low on both.

pie.ground_truth_caption: The pie chart shows the share of films directed by women at ten major film festivals in 2023, with each slice representing a festival's percentage. Sundance has the highest share at 42.5%, followed by Rotterdam (38.1%) and Locarno (36.7%); Berlin (35.2%) and Busan (33.4%) also sit in the mid-30s. Shares range from 16.9% at Telluride up to 42.5% at Sundance, indicating a varied distribution with several festivals below 25% (Cannes 18.4%, Venice 20.1%, TIFF 22.3%, BFI London 24.0%).
